compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Implemented (commit 6e0a361c6). region_gate.py (UNCHANGED/CHANGED/UNKNOWN + content-addressed detector); code_review_novelty.md + code-novelty reviewer (cites_prior_location redefined for source regions); code_review_novelty contract = kernel novelty_model; apply_region_gated_floor + score_code_novelty (reuses rising_floor_drop/reshape_novelties/decide.novelty, surfaces matched_prior_id for carried_from); gate_dispatch wires floor before emit keyed session/change (added CodeReviewRequest.change_id); reuses novelty_drop_active+2 thresholds, no new config. Local: ruff+mypy green, 63 code-review tests pass incl neutrality + region states + drop/raise/carryover/failsafe. Awaiting Gerrit Verified before close.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Pushed to Gerrit as change 477 (stacked on 476). Awaiting Verified + LLM-Review.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
This is the convergence mechanism itself: reuse plan-review's novelty machinery to stop code review
re-nagging carryover findings and to drop novel low-impact findings on UNCHANGED code, while ALWAYS
raising novel findings on newly-changed code (the per-citation region gate is the deliberate
divergence from plan-review's whole-artifact floor).

## What
- **Consume the reader.** Call `latest_code_review_result(key)` (revenued-thickset-dassie) for the
  current review's typed key to obtain prior SURFACED findings + the prior `deps` map. Key selection:
  `session:<id>` for local reviews (set on the verdict by paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish),
  `change:<id>` for Gerrit.
- **REUSE unchanged kernel primitives (no fork):** the novelty SCORING CONTRACT
  (`review_kernel.verify.novelty_model` / `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS`), the reshaper
  `review_kernel.verify.reshape_novelties`, the per-finding novelty math
  `review_kernel.decide.novelty`, and the drop predicate
  `review_kernel.decide.rising_floor_drop(priority, novelty, *, t_novel, floor)` — all verbatim. A
  thin `score_code_novelty` (mirroring plan-review's `_score_floor_novelty` runner wiring) drives
  the sub-call and, from `reshape_novelties`, surfaces BOTH the novelty float (for the drop) AND the
  `matched_prior_id` (for `carried_from`). NOTE: it does NOT reuse the kernel `score_novelty`
  wrapper verbatim — that returns only `{index: float}` and DISCARDS `matched_prior_id`, which the
  carryover-labeling AC needs; `score_code_novelty` reuses the same underlying primitives
  (reshape + decide.novelty) so the scoring is identical, just also returning the matched id.
- **NEW code-review novelty PROMPT + reviewer id.** Add `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code_review_novelty.md`
  (`outputs: code_review_novelty`, `execution_mode: single_turn`, `category: code-review-pass`) and a
  `code-novelty` reviewer id used in the sub-call's `RunRequest(reviewers=["code-novelty"])`. Wiring
  mirrors plan-review's `_score_floor_novelty` runner: the diff is the domain context and the prior
  SURFACED findings are appended to the instructions as a context block (NOT a `{{plan}}`/template
  var). Register the reused contract at `code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()`
  (`contracts.register_contract("code_review_novelty", novelty_model)`, importing `novelty_model`
  from `review_kernel.verify`) — mirroring `plan_review/passes.py`; without it a
  `RunRequest(output_schema="code_review_novelty")` fails at runtime.
  - **cites_prior_location REDEFINED for code (critical).** `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` shares the field
    name `cites_prior_location`, whose kernel/plan meaning is "same PLAN location/section/AC line".
    The code prompt MUST firmly redefine it so the model does not anchor on the plan meaning:
    `cites_prior_location — does this finding point at the SAME source file AND approximately the
    same line region (within ~10 lines) as the prior finding? (not merely the same file — the same
    localized area)`. (This closes the epic-review advisory T1.)
- **NEW `region_gate.py`** (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/region_gate.py`) defining the region-state
  vocabulary + detector:
  - Constants: `REGION_UNCHANGED = "unchanged"`, `REGION_CHANGED = "changed"`, `REGION_UNKNOWN = "unknown"`.
  - **Citation basis:** a finding's cited region is derived from its `location` field (a `path` or
    `path:line`). The detector takes the finding's cited FILE path, hashes the current file
    (`attest._hash_file`), and compares to `prior_deps[path]`: equal → `UNCHANGED`; differ → `CHANGED`;
    path absent from `prior_deps`, OR the finding is multi-location / absence-evidence (no concrete
    file) / moved / renamed / any error → `UNKNOWN`. File-level in v1 (line-level deferred).
- **NEW `region_gated_floor`** (in `code_review/workflow_ops.py`, applied over the decided advisory
  set in the produce path before the sidecar emit — the code-review analogue of plan-review's
  `_maybe_apply_rising_floor`, which sits after the verdict and before emit):
  - A finding is DROPPED iff `rising_floor_drop(priority, novelty, t_novel, floor)` is true AND its
    region is `REGION_UNCHANGED`. `REGION_CHANGED` and `REGION_UNKNOWN` NEVER drop (both raise).
  - **Thresholds/activation (decided):** reuse the EXISTING config — `verify.novelty_drop_threshold`
    (t_novel), `verify.novelty_priority_floor` (floor), AND `verify.novelty_drop_active` as the
    activation/evidence gate. This introduces NO NEW config key (the epic's "no eval-gated
    activation flag" = no NEW flag): the code-review floor reuses plan-review's SAME three keys, so
    it is OFF by default and an operator opts in with evidence exactly as plan-review requires
    (preserving the opt-in-with-evidence discipline the reviewer flagged). On top of the flag, the
    floor also SELF-GATES: inert whenever there is no prior memory for the current key (first review,
    or a reader error → empty prior set). Both gates must be open to drop anything.
  - Dropped findings carry `drop_reason = "novelty-region"`.
  - **Carryover labeling:** a finding that matches a prior surfaced finding (low novelty) but is NOT
    dropped is stamped `carried_from` = the `matched_prior_id` returned by `score_code_novelty` for
    that finding (the prior finding's id; empty ⇒ not stamped). Data model: by the time the floor runs (post-decide, in the produce path), findings
    are plain dicts in the verdict's `advisory`/`blocking` lists — `carried_from` is added as a KEY
    on that dict (no Pydantic model change needed; the coach already ran in-workflow, so the floor
    strips coaching notes whose `finding_refs` point at a `carried_from`-stamped finding from the
    verdict's `coaching` list). The finding remains surfaced.
  - **FAIL-SAFE (full mirror of plan-review):** the whole floor is wrapped in try/except; any
    reader/hash/compare/novelty error returns the FULL unfiltered finding set (no drops). A finding
    with no novelty entry defaults to novelty 0.0 (carryover = kept).
- **Neutrality (ADR 0008 Invariant 1):** the Pass-1 finder NEVER receives prior findings; only the
  post-find novelty seam does.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` (region_gated_floor + novelty sub-call wiring),
  `src/rebar/llm/code_review/region_gate.py` (NEW: vocab + detector),
  `src/rebar/llm/code_review/contracts.py` (register `code_review_novelty`),
  `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code_review_novelty.md` (NEW prompt),
  `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` (invoke the floor before emit).
- Depends on the reader/payload (revenued-thickset-dassie) and, for end-to-end LOCAL integration, on
  local persistence (paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish). This story is reviewed/implemented AFTER c639
  (+3bff) are committed on the stack, so the reader (`latest_code_review_result`) + `deps` map exist
  in the tree; region_gated_floor UNIT tests run against a mocked reader.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A `code-novelty` reviewer prompt (`code_review_novelty.md`) + reviewer id exist, binding the
      reused novelty contract, with diff context + prior findings in the instructions block and
      `cites_prior_location` redefined for source-file/line regions.
- [ ] The `code_review_novelty` contract is registered in `contracts.register_contracts()`;
      `rising_floor_drop`, `reshape_novelties`, and `decide.novelty` are reused UNCHANGED (the thin
      `score_code_novelty` surfaces `matched_prior_id` for carryover; it does not reuse the
      `score_novelty` wrapper, which discards it).
- [ ] `region_gate.py` defines `REGION_UNCHANGED`/`REGION_CHANGED`/`REGION_UNKNOWN` used by the
      detector, which compares a cited file's current sha256 to the prior `deps` map.
- [ ] `region_gated_floor` drops a finding ONLY when novel AND low-priority AND `REGION_UNCHANGED`;
      `REGION_CHANGED` and `REGION_UNKNOWN` both raise; dropped findings carry
      `drop_reason = "novelty-region"`.
- [ ] Thresholds/activation reuse the EXISTING `verify.novelty_drop_threshold`,
      `verify.novelty_priority_floor`, and `verify.novelty_drop_active` (no NEW config key); the
      floor is OFF by default and additionally self-gates inert with no prior memory.
- [ ] Carryover findings are stamped `carried_from` = the matched prior finding's id (string, added
      as a dict key), have their coaching notes stripped from the verdict, but remain surfaced.
- [ ] Any reader/hash/compare/novelty error returns the full unfiltered finding set (no drops).
- [ ] The Pass-1 finder receives no prior findings; only the novelty seam does — asserted by a test
      that the finder/verifier pass inputs contain no prior-findings context (ADR 0008 Invariant 1).
- [ ] Tests (mocked reader): unchanged-region low-priority novel → dropped; changed-region novel →
      raised; absence/multi-location → UNKNOWN → raised; carryover → `carried_from` = matched id +
      coaching stripped; forced reader error → no drops; and a neutrality test that the Pass-1
      finder step receives no prior findings.
compiled_state.title: region_gated_floor: content-addressed novelty convergence for code review
